     The Numbers Business Report

A Star is Born (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $215,288,866Details
International Box Office $221,100,000
Worldwide Box Office $436,388,866
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $23,634,450
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $28,272,896
Total $51,907,346
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Jackson Maine discovers—and falls in love with—struggling artist Ally. She has just about given up on her dream to make it big as a singer… until Jack coaxes her into the spotlight. But even as Ally’s career takes off, the personal side of their relationship is breaking down, as Jack fights an ongoing battle with his own internal demons.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$42,908,051 (19.9% of total gross)
Legs:5.02 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:49.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$36,000,000 (worldwide box office is 12.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,686 opening theaters/3,904 max. theaters, 9.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $282,539,225

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 5th, 2018 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: October 5th, 2018 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
October 5th, 2018 (Wide) (France)
October 5th, 2018 (Wide) (Germany)
October 5th, 2018 (Wide), released as Ha nacido una estrella (Spain)
October 5th, 2018 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
Video Release: February 19th, 2019 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for language throughout, some sexuality/nudity and substance abuse.
(Rating bulletin 2501 (Cert #51308), 11/8/2017)
Running Time: 140 minutes
Award nomination: 2019 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Cast, crew, or production detail: Actors Making Their Directorial Debut, Directing Yourself, Remake, Screenplay Written By the Star
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Arizona
Plot point: Addiction, Big Break, Film Actor, Musicians, Performing Arts, Romance, Singers, Songwriter, Suicide
Social setting: Movie Business, Music Industry
Subgenre: Romantic Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Joint Effort, Warner Bros., Live Nation Productions, Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer Pictures, Jon Peters, Bill Gerber
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Jackson Maine discovers—and falls in love with—struggling artist Ally. She has just about given up on her dream to make it big as a singer… until Jack coaxes her into the spotlight. But even as Ally’s career takes off, the personal side of their relationship is breaking down, as Jack fights an ongoing battle with his own internal demons.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$42,908,051 (19.9% of total gross)
Legs:5.02 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:49.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$36,000,000 (worldwide box office is 12.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,686 opening theaters/3,904 max. theaters, 9.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $282,539,225

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 5th, 2018 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: October 5th, 2018 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
October 5th, 2018 (Wide) (France)
October 5th, 2018 (Wide) (Germany)
October 5th, 2018 (Wide), released as Ha nacido una estrella (Spain)
October 5th, 2018 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
Video Release: February 19th, 2019 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for language throughout, some sexuality/nudity and substance abuse.
(Rating bulletin 2501 (Cert #51308), 11/8/2017)
Running Time: 140 minutes
Award nomination: 2019 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Cast, crew, or production detail: Actors Making Their Directorial Debut, Directing Yourself, Remake, Screenplay Written By the Star
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Arizona
Plot point: Addiction, Big Break, Film Actor, Musicians, Performing Arts, Romance, Singers, Songwriter, Suicide
Social setting: Movie Business, Music Industry
Subgenre: Romantic Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Joint Effort, Warner Bros., Live Nation Productions, Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer Pictures, Jon Peters, Bill Gerber
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
